response.justification: Aisha Okonkwo is a accomplished full-stack and systems engineer with strong fundamentals, but the role demands deep C++ expertise as its core requirement — her C++ exposure is limited to approximately six months at National Instruments and a bachelor's thesis project. Her primary stack is TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang, and there is no evidence of multi-threaded C++ systems, STL usage, message-broker work in a C++ context, or industrial/IoT domain knowledge. Critical security competencies (IEC 62443, SDL) and relevant protocols (MQTT, OPC UA, Modbus) are entirely absent from her history.
strengths: Demonstrated high-performance systems engineering: built low-latency micro-services handling millions of HTTP requests per second at Dunnhumby using async I/O and fiber-based concurrency
Hands-on event-driven and distributed architecture experience via the GISCollective geo-data platform and direct mail automation work at Optilyz
Strong software-craftsmanship culture: practitioner of TDD, trunk-based development, and active technical mentor through Refactoring Dojos, Code Katas, and public workshops
concerns: C++ experience is superficial — roughly six months at National Instruments writing components for LabWindows/CVI and one thesis project; C++ does not appear in her listed skills
No exposure to IEC 62443, Secure Development Lifecycle, or any industrial cybersecurity standards required by the role
No evidence of industrial automation, IoT protocols (MQTT, Modbus, OPC UA, IEC 61850), or embedded/on-premises platform development anywhere in the resume
